verdicts.reasoning: ConnectionTest.php:291 - The value is wrapped in intval() which casts to integer, making XSS impossible. Additionally, the render_admin_page() method is only accessible via an admin menu page registered with 'manage_woocommerce' capability (line 86/97), so authentication IS required despite the scanner marking auth_required=false. The admin_init hook (line 63) also requires WordPress admin login.

ConnectionTest.php:309 - The value is wrapped in esc_url() which is the correct sanitizer for URL output in an HTML attribute. esc_url() strips dangerous protocols (javascript:, data:) and encodes special characters. Additionally requires 'manage_woocommerce' capability to access the page.
ConnectionTest.php:312 - Same pattern as xss-001: intval() casts to integer, no XSS possible. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.

ConnectionTest.php:365 - The $_GET['account_id'] is used inside esc_url(wp_nonce_url(add_query_arg(...))). esc_url() properly sanitizes the output for HTML context. The account_id is used as a query arg value which gets URL-encoded by add_query_arg(). Requires manage_woocommerce capability.

ConnectionTest.php:374 - The expression `($_GET['account_id'] ?? false) || $merchant_id` is used as a boolean condition. The output is either an empty string '' or the hardcoded string 'disabled="disabled" title="Missing account ID"'. The user input is never echoed; only its truthiness determines which hardcoded string to output. No XSS possible.

ConnectionTest.php:383 - Same pattern as xss-004: $_GET values are used inside esc_url(wp_nonce_url(add_query_arg(...))). esc_url() is the correct sanitizer and properly handles all dangerous characters for HTML output. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.
ConnectionTest.php:392 - Identical pattern to xss-005: boolean check on $_GET['account_id'] truthiness outputs only hardcoded strings. No user input reaches output.
ConnectionTest.php:453 - intval() casts to integer, impossible to inject XSS via an integer value. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.
ConnectionTest.php:574 - intval() casts to integer, impossible to inject XSS. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.

ConnectionTest.php:577 - The expression `! empty($_GET['async']) ? 'checked' : ''` uses user input only as a boolean condition. Output is either the hardcoded string 'checked' or empty string. No user data reaches output.
ConnectionTest.php:584 - intval() casts to integer. No XSS possible. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.
ConnectionTest.php:595 - Boolean check on $_GET['async'], outputs only hardcoded 'checked' or ''. No user data in output.
ConnectionTest.php:602 - intval() casts to integer. No XSS possible. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.
ConnectionTest.php:613 - Boolean check on $_GET['async'], outputs only hardcoded 'checked' or ''. No user data in output.
ConnectionTest.php:621 - intval() casts to integer. No XSS possible. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.
ConnectionTest.php:632 - Boolean check on $_GET['async'], outputs only hardcoded 'checked' or ''. No user data in output.
ConnectionTest.php:640 - intval() casts to integer. No XSS possible. Requires manage_woocommerce capability.

ConnectionTest.php:766-779 - The $_GET['manager_id'] is passed through absint() (line 766) which returns an unsigned integer. This integer is used as a POST body parameter 'managerId' (line 772), NOT as part of the URL. The URL for wp_remote_post() comes from get_connect_server_url() which is hardcoded/configured, not user-controlled. The user can only influence an integer value in the request body. Additionally, check_admin_referer('wcs-google-manager') is called (line 760), requiring admin auth + nonce.

ConnectionTest.php:790 - The wp_redirect($json['oauthUrl']) redirects to a URL from the JSON response of the plugin's own WooCommerce Connect Server (line 779). The URL being POSTed to is hardcoded via get_connect_server_url() (line 767). The server response is trusted infrastructure. The user can only influence the integer 'managerId' body parameter (via absint()). An attacker cannot control the oauthUrl in the response. Requires admin auth + nonce (check_admin_referer on line 760).

JetpackWPCOM.php:103-106 - The $_SERVER['HTTP_HOST'] value is passed through set_url_scheme() then esc_url() before being output in an HTML attribute value='...'. esc_url() is the correct sanitizer for this context: it strips dangerous protocols, encodes HTML special characters (&, <, >, ', "), and validates the URL structure. The output is inside a single-quoted HTML attribute and esc_url() encodes single quotes. Additionally, HTTP_HOST is typically validated by the web server and cannot contain arbitrary HTML/JS in practice.

summary: All 20 scanner findings for google-listings-and-ads are false positives. The ConnectionTest.php findings (18 of 20) all target a debug/testing admin page that requires 'manage_woocommerce' capability and uses nonce verification on all actions. The XSS findings are sanitized via intval() (integer casting), esc_url(), or only use user input as boolean conditions outputting hardcoded strings. The SSRF finding incorrectly treats an integer POST body parameter as URL injection. The open redirect finding misidentifies a trusted server response URL as user-controlled. The JetpackWPCOM.php XSS finding is properly sanitized with esc_url().
feedback: Scanner has a 100% false positive rate on this plugin. Major issues: (1) Scanner does not recognize intval() as a sanitizer for XSS - integers cannot contain HTML/JS. (2) Scanner does not recognize esc_url() as a sanitizer for XSS in URL/attribute contexts. (3) Scanner marks all findings as auth_required=false despite the code being inside WordPress admin pages requiring manage_woocommerce capability. (4) Scanner flags boolean conditionals as taint flows even when only hardcoded strings are output. (5) Scanner conflates POST body parameter injection with SSRF URL injection. Recommendation: Add intval/absint to the sanitizer allowlist, recognize add_menu_page/add_submenu_page capability requirements as auth checks, and distinguish between URL parameters and POST body values for SSRF detection.
